input.testCaseTitle: Refund webhook receives refund.retry.exhausted — handler timeout
input.stepLabel: Wait for refund.retry.exhausted webhook delivery — expect handler 200 within 30s
input.errorMessage: TimeoutError: Webhook handler did not respond within 30000ms. Expected POST /webhooks/payment-gateway → 200. Gateway retry log shows 3 attempts at 0s/10s/20s with last attempt status: timeout. Final refund.retry.exhausted event was queued at 20.3s but handler never acknowledged.
kbContext: Render Free Hobby dynos cold-start at 15-min idle. Webhook receiver hosted on staging-iksula uses same dyno; first request after idle takes up to 30s to respond.
Payment gateway sends retries with 10s spacing; if handler is cold-booting, the entire retry budget can be exhausted before handler responds.

groundTruth.rootCauseDetail: Render Free Hobby dyno cold-start interaction with payment-gateway webhook retry policy. Dyno was idle >15min; first webhook hit the cold dyno which took 28s to boot, leaving 2s for the actual handler → all 3 gateway retries timed out before handler responded. UptimeRobot 4-min keep-alive on /health should prevent this (per R001 in M4 v2 plan).
groundTruth.notesForEval: Top-2 hit on env-config / flaky-network / payment-gateway. Prior-history pass-rate of 40% is a strong flake signal. The 'real' root cause is the infrastructure config (cold-start window), but a reviewer focused on the gateway retry behavior could call it payment-gateway, and a reviewer focused on the symptomatic timing could call it flaky-network. All three are acceptable.
